title: Telis ovalis
taxonomy: Telis ovalis is a species of fungus in the family Phyllachoraceae. It belongs to the genus Telis and is classified as a type of ascomycete fungus. Ascomycete fungi are a large and diverse group of organisms, with many species found in various environments around the world. Telis ovalis is likely to be found in a specific type of habitat, such as a forest or grassland ecosystem. Further research would be needed to determine the precise classification and characteristics of this particular species.

intro: Telis ovalis, also known as Oval-leaf Telis, is a plant species within the family Asteraceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Telis ovalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Telis ovalis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and ointments, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions.
